Performance-based budgeting is the practice of developing 
budgets based on the relationship between program funding levels and expected results or anticipated outcome from that program.  Performance-based budgeting is the allocation of funds based on programmatic results that contribute to organizational goals. It refers to a single or a set of performance target(s) which must be achieved at a given expenditure level
. 
NEED FOR PERFORMANCE-BASED BUDGETING
•	Shortens the lengthy, traditional budgeting and 
reporting procedures, which saves money and time simultaneously.
•	Improve the transparency and accountability of government through strengthening the link between resources and services delivered. Budgets of programs linked to specific work plans and outputs. 
•	Integrate the recurrent and development budgets, therefore making it easier to see the interplay between recurrent, and capital expenditure in achieving the outputs. 
•	Improve the monitoring of the budget since the outputs and performance indicators are specific and identified prior to the budgeting process. •	Enables greater linkage between budget outlays and performance of institutions.
•	Crucial to developing sustainable fiscal policies and economic growth
•	Decision makers reach better long- and short-term budget decisions; stake- holders see more visible performance measures, timelier information, and easier identification of actual strategic priorities; and, in the process, organizations build more solid growth models, lag variances between costs and expected outcomes.ABOUT PBS

TECHNO BRAIN PBS SYSTEM
The Performance Based Budgeting System (PBBS) is a 
computerized budgeting tool designed to help automate and streamline traditional budgeting processes as per Performance Based Budgeting guidelines. BENEFITS
10 Basic Benefits of PBS system
1. PBB improves cost-efficiency and manages 
effective budgeting outlays, increasing visibility into how government policies translate into spending and making systematic use of performance information. 
2. PBS Dashboard improves business performance and 
drives business behavior by providing critical insights for quicker analysis, decision-making and execution. 
3. Eliminates manual consolidation - Including budget 
frameworks papers, estimates, and budget execution reports across ministries.
4. Built to be Customizable and Adaptable - allows 
you to configure the software to best suit your needs with the means to adapt to your unique requirements. We continually develop our software to ensure the ongoing evolution of relevant functionality and governance pertinent to your particular operation. 
5. Simplify integration- PBS provides a deeper level of functional integration and is simpler and less costly to maintain. Real time exchange of budgetary data with others systems such as IFMIS and other HRMS Systems. 
6. Reduce cost of ownership. We offer the source code for the client to fully own the system. Less dependency on the developer if there are changes within the budget framework. 
7. Provides access to historical budget data by users 
within Ministerial Agencies and Institutions
8. Enable effective security - PBS incorporates a 
number of facilities to prevent unauthorized access and ensure authentication, enabling companies to implement internal controls according to their specific governance requirements. The entire system is protected by various security levels and access controls. These include operator logins and passwords, organizational role access and user interface settings, access levels for organizations.
9. Facilitate the preparation of budget documents 
i.e. Budget Framework Paper, Estimates, PIP , Output Budget, Performance Contracts, Ministerial Policy Statements, and Procurement Plans. 
10. Ease of Deployment- PBS gives you the flexibility to choose the deployment that best suits your unique process flow needs and the ability to choose the device that best meets the task. Accessible from anywhere over the Internet; available on iPad / smart phones PBBS provides public sector organizations to automate 
all their core budget activities to operate efficiently, transparently and improve productivity, while meeting complex regulatory, budgetary requirements for effective accountability and service delivery, such as better strategic planning, effective cash flow control, a complete budget rules compliance, enhanced citizen services and document security.


OVERVIEW
      
1. Workflow Mechanism - PBS support 10 level and 
above user hierarchy for Workflow, which provides the capability to design and enable the streamlined flow of processes through a fluid and user-friendly interface. We provide a set of tools to orchestrate their own workflows according to their business processes, simply and effectively. 
2. Interface with Third Party Systems -  PBS can work alongside with any modern IFMIS (SAP , Microsoft Dynamics, Oracle, EPICOR) solution
3. Role-based Workspaces - PBS provides users with the complete tools and information needed to perform their role efficiently and effectively, displaying information most relevant to a user’s touch points. Improves security management by restricting user access appropriately and delineating roles and responsibilities on the system among users 4. Dynamic generation of Budget Books - PBBS is highly reliable and scalable up to hundreds of thousands of transactions per second and eases the Macro level reporting by capturing both financial and non-financial budget data and budget books, consolidated for about 1800 pages. 
5. PBS Web-Based Interface - Whether deployed on premise, in the cloud, or both (hybrid), PBS gives you the choice and flexibility to quickly and easily access the solution through a web browser, no matter where you are. 
6. Multi-level Authentication – only authenticated users can login to the application using a high level - JWT authentication. 
1. PLANNING
2. PREPARATION
3. APPROVAL4. EXECUTION5. REPORTS
BUDGETING•	 Both Top-down and Bottom-up Approach 
•	 Configurable Budgeting forecasting (3 Year & 5 Years)
•	 Budget Planning – Framework Process
•	 Budget Preparation – Draft Estimation
•	 Budget Approval – Budget Submission
•	 Budget Reporting – Performance Evaluation and other Analytical reports
•	 Open Architecture
•	 Light weight using client side technologies
•	  Integrate with Mobile

•	 Budget Reporting – Mobile Integration


OVERVIEW
Sample Budget Books:
1. Budget Framework Paper  
2. Draft Budget Estimates
3. Approved Budget Estimates  
4. Ministerial Policy Statements5. MDA Performance Contract 
6. Quarterly Progress Reports 
7. Semi/ Annual Budget 
8. Performance Public Investment PlanConsolidation of Budget Book
1. All the financial and non-financial budget data can 
be developed from PBBS which eases the submission. PBBS supports ad hoc report generation using BI tool built in within PBBS. 
2. Audit trail to track user activities in the system
3. Budget Reporting – Quarterly Progress and Performance Evaluation and other Analytical reports
4. Interface with Third Party Systems like IFMIS (SAP/Oracle/Epicor), HRMS, Donor Systems and etc.
PBS Dashboards - PBS dashboard provides interactive 
visual representations of real-time data in the form of clear pictorial representations, such as pie diagrams and graphs enabling managers to measure and monitor performance, and conduct what-if modelling.  It is an analytical dashboard where user can view the data at a single glance of what is happening at a particular time, or drill down to the finest details.
